text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by predicting the next word in a sequence based on patterns learned from vast amounts of training data, such as books, articles, and websites. This data helps them recognize grammar, facts, and even some reasoning. When you use an LLM, you give it a prompt—a question or instruction—and it responds based on its training. This is called prompt engineering, where careful wording can improve results. LLMs have impressive capabilities, like writing essays or answering questions, but they have limits. They can make mistakes, lack true understanding, and sometimes produce incorrect or biased information.
